title: linaria supina subsp. nevadensis
intro: Linaria supina subsp. nevadensis, also known as the Nevada toadflax or Nevada linaria, is a subspecies of the Linaria supina plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the figwort family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Linaria supina subsp. nevadensis include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or by creating a decoction with the roots, and it is also used in some parts of the world as a food ingredient, particularly in traditional soups.
